principle: Every corpus document in a supported format must run through a shipping convert-to-Markdown pathway. Conversion is native-only (no Python); formats that had only a Python/Docling engine are listed in unsupported_formats and are not required to have a shipping pathway.
unsupported_formats_note: These formats were served only by the removed Python/Docling runtime (AsciiDoc, NLM/JATS nxml, LaTeX, WebVTT, generic XML) and have no native engine. Their corpus documents are retained as historical reference and are exempt from shipping-pathway coverage. Add a native engine and a pathway here to bring one back into scope.

swift-pdfkit.notes: Apple-native digital PDF extraction path. Uses PDFKit text extraction plus small Markdown cleanup heuristics.

swift-vision-document.accelerator_control: none; Vision chooses available Apple hardware internally
swift-vision-document.notes: Apple-native OCR, document analysis, image quality, and classification path (with adaptive table banding).

swift-speech.accelerator_control: none; Speech framework chooses available Apple hardware internally
swift-speech.availability: Speech authorization and recognizer availability gated

swift-office.notes: Native OOXML/legacy-binary Office conversion via the vendored SwiftOfficeMarkdown engine (ZipReader + part parsers). Replaces the Python MarkItDown/Docling office path.

swift-html.notes: In-process HTML-to-Markdown via libxml2/XMLDocument. Replaces the Python MarkItDown HTML path.

swift-epub.notes: In-process EPUB-to-Markdown: SwiftOfficeMarkdown ZipReader + OPF spine ordering + the native HTML walker. Replaces the Python MarkItDown EPUB path.
